page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The CPU features a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for matrix multiplication. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data reveals a clear division between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance achieves average performance in single-core metrics, such as a stress-ng single-core score of 1620.59 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1635.0. However, multi-core and memory-bound operations are notably weaker. The Geekbench multi-core score of 3703.0 and the Passmark memory score of 989.87 fall into the bottom tiers. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with uncached read and write speeds of 27.54 GB/sec and 21.03 GB/sec respectively.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-highcpu-4 offers a low-memory configuration that reduces overhead for workloads that do not require large memory footprints. It is suited for single-threaded processing, basic text processing, static web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference using small models, where it achieves 393.48 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a 135M model. It is less suited for memory-intensive databases, software compilation, or highly parallelized multi-core applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor at 2.7 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core execution, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1620.59 and stable static web serving throughput of 6.78 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench multi-core score of 3703.0 and a low Passmark memory score of 989.87. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for CPU-bound tasks with low memory requirements, such as single-threaded applications, small-scale LLM inference, and basic web serving, but is not recommended for memory-heavy databases or highly parallel workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, offering average single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-4 features 4 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and Intel Xeon architecture, optimized for low-memory, single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated Intel Xeon compute instance with a low memory-to-CPU ratio, optimized for single-threaded tasks and lightweight web serving.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4-vCPU Intel Xeon 8481C processor at 2.7 GHz
Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM and no bundled local storage
Supports Intel AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
Delivers average single-core performance but exhibits weak multi-core scaling
Best suited for single-threaded tasks, static web serving, and small LLM inference
